fingerprint_method: sha256 of .context/config.json bytes; sha256 of the concatenation of .context/schemas/*.json in lexicographic filename order; byte fingerprints only, no semantic read

rules.if: atom has an entry in atom_facts with disposition set
rules.then: no filing; record the disposition and its citation in the run ledger
rules.because: a later user statement already disposed the atom; the record is authority (no-atemporal-record-reading policy, 2026-07-22)

rules.if: atom_facts.cited_id names an existing substrate item (prefix map: FGAP->framework-gaps, TASK->tasks, FEAT->features, DEC->decisions, VER->verification, R->research, issue->issues, PHASE->phase)
rules.mode: relate-or-update the named item; never append a sibling
rules.because: prior-art precondition: the substrate already tracks the ground; item counts only grow, unguarded filing is where duplicates accumulate (CLAUDE.md explore-before-file)

the record's content IS an instance of an installed kind; the kind's own description names the content (rationale: 'the deeper reasoning behind decisions'; session-notes: 'per-session handoff records: focus, discoveries, questions, decisions made, current status, and next steps')
framework-gaps description: 'pieces that must exist natively ... what the framework cannot yet express at all, not bugs in expressible functionality'

rules.if: atom_facts.defect_kind = bug-in-expressible AND evidence carries a file:line location
rules.because: issues description: 'Known bugs, missing capabilities, design debt, and open work items ... code location, package scope'
research description: 'what was investigated, how, what was found, what grounds the findings'

rules.if: atom_facts.parent_artifact names an existing FGAP/FEAT/issue/PHASE item
rules.mode: append with birth relations: task_addresses_* -> parent_artifact, plus item_governed_by_convention or item_acknowledges_missing_convention (installed invariant task-articulates-convention is ERROR severity; a bare task is refused)
rules.because: tasks description: 'The operational layer between gaps/requirements and code changes'; observed convention: this substrate's tasks are born citing a forcing parent (21 task titles name FGAP-/FEAT-/issue- ids)

rules.if: atom_facts.split = true (the atom carries two facts landing on two kinds)
rules.then: two filings, one per half, each placed by re-running R2-R6 on its half; cross-linked by relation
rules.because: neither contract is satisfiable by one item; splitting before placement is the mechanical resolution (instance: SA-18 regen-mechanism gap + regen work task)

rules.if: no prior rule fires, or a required fact line is absent, or a cited/parent id does not resolve
rules.then: exception-queue row with reason code (unmapped | missing-fact | target-not-found | no-substrate-home); run continues
rules.because: never default, never guess, never stop the batch for one row; unplaceable records are data, not crashes

recognitions/substrate-conflicts kinds not built; the gap item's content is exactly the missing-native-piece class
no substrate home exists for project-identity content; framework-gaps is the meta-carrier for a missing home
